title: Woodsia alpina
taxonomy: Woodsia alpina is classified within the family Woodsiaceae, which is a family of ferns commonly known as the wood ferns. This family is placed within the order Polypodiales, which is a group of ferns that have leaflets arranged in a single plane. The class Polypodiopsida, to which Woodsia alpina belongs, is a group of vascular plants that includes all types of ferns. At the phylum level, it is classified as a member of the Polypodiophyta, which is a group of vascular plants that includes ferns and their allies. The kingdom Plantae is the broadest classification for Woodsia alpina, encompassing all types of flowering plants and non-flowering plants, including ferns.
uses: Woodsia alpina, a species of fern native to the Himalayas and the Tibetan Plateau, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The plant's leaves and stems are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties. Traditionally, Woodsia alpina is used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. Its extracts have also been studied for their potential in treating cancer and other diseases. However, further research is needed to confirm the plant's efficacy and safety for medicinal use.
